raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint against Barclays for a failure to properly investigate the XXXX XXXX breach of contract and deceptive trade practices under XXXX Reason Code XXXX ( Merchandise Not as Described ). 
I purchased XXXX  from XXXX XXXX whose policy states a California return address and prepaid shipping for defective goods. The item arrived defective. In good faith, I agreed to a replacement. The replacement was also defective. Under XXXX rules, a failed replacement constitutes a failed remedy, entitling the consumer to a full refund. 
XXXX XXXX is demanding an international return to XXXX, contradicting their official California policy. Furthermore, XXXX XXXX shipped the second defective item from New York, proving they have domestic logistics but are using an international shipping requirement as an 'unreasonable hurdle ' to prevent a refund. 
Barclays closed my dispute based on 'Proof of Delivery. ' I am not disputing delivery ; I am disputing that the items are defective and XXXX XXXX  is refusing to honor their written return policy. Barclays has ignored the evidence of the second defect and the NY shipping origin. 
Barclays violated the " Unreasonable Hurdle '' rule by allowing XXXX XXXX to demand an international return when a domestic one was promised. 
When I attempted to resolve this, a Barclays supervisor hung up the phone after stating the case was closed, refusing to consider the new evidence of the second defective item.
